assumption_text: The civilian footprint is the main Panjshir Valley plus inhabited side valleys and cave/refuge areas inside the valley-system, not all of Parwan/Panjshir Province and not refugee camps in Pakistan.
rationale: Both Girardet and Dowell/Girardet describe civilians sheltering in side valleys rather than instantly disappearing from the theater.

assumption_text: The military denominator includes Soviet/DRA formations inserted into or advancing through Panjshir and mujahideen reinforcements directly fighting there, but excludes national forces outside the valley campaign.
rationale: Project rules forbid using whole-country military populations when only a subset of forces were actually committed.

assumption_text: Average civilian presence declined over the May-September campaign from roughly 80,000-95,000 toward lower late-1982 levels, producing scenario averages of 50,000 / 70,000 / 80,000 civilians present.
rationale: This interpolates between Girardet's 1980 baseline, Dowell/Girardet's mid-1982 80,000 residents, and Girardet's late-1982 surviving 40,000-50,000 inhabitants.

assumption_text: Peak force claims are converted into effective combatant person-months rather than assuming full peak strength remained present and equally exposed every day of the campaign.
rationale: Sources provide peak or attack-package counts and broad durations, not daily presence logs.

assumption_text: The full estimate uses 1982-05-17 to 1982-09-30 as the active campaign window even though ceasefire maneuvering continued later in 1982.
rationale: Girardet's campaign-death totals appear to cover the fifth and sixth offensives and their immediate aftermath, while direct combat had largely petered out by late 1982.

assumption_text: Girardet's 'well over 2,000 dead and wounded' and '1,200 Afghan soldiers killed, captured or defected' are excluded from the core direct-death numerator except as plausibility context.
rationale: Project rules prohibit converting mixed casualty language into deaths unless the source cleanly supports that transformation.

assumption_text: Unknown-status deaths are left unallocated and the DR uses strict exclusion rather than proportional allocation.
rationale: No separate unknown-status Panjshir 1982 series was found.
